associations: longest common subsequence -> hidden markov models -> sequence alignment -> bioinformatics -> entropy rate -> directed polymers -> random medium -> spin glass -> replica trick
Chvatal-Sankoff -> kingman subadditive -> percolation -> first passage percolation -> metric shape -> Tracy-Widom -> KPZ
blind_spots: Assuming the constant \gamma_2 can be found in closed form; it may be irreducible or transcendental.
Relying too heavily on asymptotic scaling (KPZ) without rigorous non-asymptotic error bounds for small n.
Ignoring that the combinatorial state space grows exponentially, making exact finite calculations hit a hard computational wall at n~30 (unless using deep branch and bound like Heineman et al.).
anomaly_results: Tiskin's initial claim of an exact algebraic form was retracted (v2 of arxiv:2212.01582), implying infinite-state transitions likely inject chaotic/transcendental parameters.
Finite size scaling \gamma_n = \gamma_2 + O(n^{-c}) exhibits a very strong finite-size effect, rendering naive Monte Carlo simulations virtually useless for bounds tighter than 0.01.
